# Independent Watchmaking

> A living catalogue of 346 independent watchmakers, watch brands, manufactures, and microbrands worldwide — curated by @Dante.Miami. Each listing includes founding date, location, annual production volume, price range, official website, social handles, and a detailed history of the maker.

Independent Watchmaking (https://independentwatchmaking.com/) is a free, regularly updated reference directory covering the full spectrum of independent horology: legendary masters (F.P. Journe, Philippe Dufour, Voutilainen, MB&F), AHCI members, one-person artisan workshops, historic manufactures, accessible microbrands, and emerging independents from every region — Switzerland, Germany, Japan, China, Finland, Russia, the United States, the United Kingdom, Austria, and beyond.

## What this site covers

- Independent watchmakers and one-person ateliers
- Independent watch brands and haute horlogerie houses
- Watch manufactures producing in-house movements
- Microbrands and accessible independents
- Enamel, guilloché, engraving, and métiers d'art specialists
- Tourbillon, constant-force, and complication innovators
- For each maker: founding year, city/country, estimated annual production, price range, official website, Instagram, and a multi-paragraph curated history

## Primary resource

- [Full catalogue](https://independentwatchmaking.com/): the complete searchable, filterable directory of all 346 makers. The complete list of makers is also embedded in the page as schema.org JSON-LD structured data (ItemList of Brand entities) readable without JavaScript execution.

## What this site does NOT cover

This catalogue is limited to genuinely independent watchmaking. A brand is excluded if it is owned by one of the dominant global luxury/watch conglomerates, if it is independently owned but has grown into a large-scale or mass-market operation whose revenue or production places it among established industrial houses rather than independent ateliers, or if its output is primarily homage watches (close copies of other brands' existing designs). Full detail and the current exclusion list are published on the site itself under "A Note on Curation" (https://independentwatchmaking.com/#curation-note). Notable exclusions include:

- **Swatch Group**: Breguet, Harry Winston, Blancpain, Glashütte Original, Jaquet Droz, Omega, Longines, Rado, Union Glashütte, Tissot, Certina, Mido, Hamilton, Balmain, Swatch, Flik Flak
- **Richemont**: A. Lange & Söhne, IWC Schaffhausen, Jaeger-LeCoultre, Panerai, Piaget, Roger Dubuis, Vacheron Constantin, Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels, Buccellati, Montblanc, Dunhill
- **LVMH**: TAG Heuer, Hublot, Zenith, Bulgari, Louis Vuitton, Dior, Tiffany & Co., Daniel Roth, Gérald Genta, L'Epée 1839, Chaumet, Fred
- **Franck Muller Watchland Group**: Franck Muller, Pierre Kunz, Backes & Strauss, Rodolphe, Alexis Barthelay, European Company Watch (ECW)
- **Fossil Group**: Fossil, Skagen, Michele, Zodiac, Relic
- **Movado Group**: Movado, Concord, Ebel, Olivia Burton, MVMT
- **Festina Group**: Festina, Lotus, Jaguar, Candino, Calypso, Perrelet, L. Leroy (considered for independent-in-spirit status, like Arnold & Son, Angelus, and Ferdinand Berthoud, but its flagship movement is built by an external Geneva supplier rather than produced in-house, so it does not currently qualify), Kronaby
- **Citychamp Watch & Jewellery Group**: Eterna, Rotary, Rossini, Ebohr, Codex, Dreyfuss & Co., J&T Windmills
- **Timex Group**: Timex, TX (Technoluxury), Ironman, Waterbury, Daniel Wellington
- **Mondaine Group**: Mondaine, Luminox, Pierre Cardin, M-Watch
- **Independently owned but too large in scale**: Rolex, Tudor, Breitling, Patek Philippe, Audemars Piguet, Richard Mille, Seiko, Grand Seiko, Credor (though Credor's Micro Artist Studio & Shizukuishi Watch Studio are included), Chopard (though Chopard-owned Ferdinand Berthoud is included), Baume & Mercier (owned by Damiani Group), Chanel, Hermès, Citizen (though Citizen-owned Arnold & Son and Angelus are included), incl. Frédérique Constant, Alpina, Casio, Invicta, Ciga Design, Victorinox, Spinnaker (owned by Dartmouth Brands), Shinola, Nixon

**Exceptions — included despite scale**: Christopher Ward and Yema both produce at a considerably larger volume than most of this catalogue, but are included because each has invested seriously in genuine in-house manufacturing — Christopher Ward with its SH21 and CW-001–003 calibers and a 2023 GPHG award for the in-house Bel Canto chiming complication; Yema with four successive generations of in-house calibers since 2009, including an in-house tourbillon (the CMM.30). By contrast, Ciga Design (~200,000 units/year, roughly 6–7x Christopher Ward and Yema's volume) is excluded: most of its models run modified third-party movements rather than extensive in-house manufacture.

**Exceptions — included despite group ownership**: Arnold & Son and Angelus (Citizen Group, via La Joux-Perret) and Ferdinand Berthoud (Chopard) are included because each operates as a genuinely autonomous manufacture — its own premises, its own team, full creative and technical control over its own movements and designs — functioning in practice like an independent house rather than a division of a larger commercial group. Credor's Micro Artist Studio and its sister atelier Shizukuishi Watch Studio (Seiko) are a further example: together a small circle of craftsmen, personally trained in finishing by Philippe Dufour, producing extremely limited pieces (as few as eight) such as the Fugaku Tourbillon.

This list is maintained on a best-effort basis and updates as brand ownership changes.

## Usage

Content may be cited with attribution to independentwatchmaking.com. The catalogue is updated regularly as new independent makers are added.
